title: Russia fears Ukrainian drones prompting Moscow mobile internet shutdown

contents:
# Russia fears Ukrainian drones prompting Moscow mobile internet shutdown

## Overview
Russia planned to switch off mobile internet in Moscow due to fears of Ukrainian drone attacks. This measure addresses threats from AI-guided drones targeting deep inside Russian territory.

## Details
Ukrainian forces conduct daily drone strikes on Russian oil refineries and infrastructure far inland. The shutdown aims to disrupt drone navigation reliant on mobile networks. Experts note this as part of broader AI and drone transformations in the Ukraine war. Russia faces ongoing missile and drone assaults despite initial expectations of quick victory. Casualties number in tens of thousands on both sides. The decision reflects escalating drone warfare tactics seen in the conflict.[2]

**Location**: Moscow, Russia

## Source Context
The information comes from Twitter @Minhdr18, which covers Chinese and Russian military hardware, weapons systems, defense technology. The reporting matches their typical area of focus.

## Significance
This development adds to the ongoing pattern of activity tracked through this source. Analysts following Chinese and Russian military hardware will likely watch for follow-up reporting.
